articles.headline: World Cup Daily LIVE: Iran despair after late Austria drama; South Africa face Canada as round of 32 begins
articles.description: The knockout stage has officially begun! The round of 32 at the 2026 World Cup begins with our first match being South Africa against co-hosts Canada in Inglewood, California.

images.name: Sasa Kalajdzic celebrates, Algeria vs Austria, 2026 FIFA World Cup [1296x729]
images.alt: Sasa Kalajdzic #14 of Austria celebrates with teammates after scoring the team's third goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group J match between Algeria and Austria at Kansas City Stadium on June 27, 2026 in Kansas City, Missouri.
images.credit: Alex Pantling - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

articles.headline: This World Cup's United States team the best in country's history - Jürgen Klopp
articles.description: Jürgen Klopp has told ESPN he believes Mauricio Pochettino's United States are the best version of the national team in the country's history.

articles.headline: Judging overreactions to World Cup bracket: How far could USMNT go?

articles.headline: 2026 World Cup Golden Boot tracker: Will Messi, Mbappe, or other score most goals?
articles.description: We're into the round of 32 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and the race for the Golden Boot -- given to the tournament's top goal scorer -- is red-hot.

images.name: Messi celeb vs. Algeria
images.caption: Lionel Messi scored a hat trick in Argentina's World Cup opener against Algeria.
images.credit: Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Kylian Mbappe celeb vs. Senegal
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé scored twice in France's World Cup opener against Senegal.
images.alt: Kylian Mbappe #10 of France celebrates scoring his team's first goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group I match between France and Senegal at New York New Jersey Stadium on June 16, 2026 in East Rutherford, New Jersey.

images.name: Erling Haaland celeb vs. Iraq
images.caption: Erling Haaland scored two goals in Norway's World Cup opener against Iraq.

images.name: Harry Kane
images.caption: Harry Kane scored twice in England's World Cup opener against Croatia.
images.credit: Maja Hitij - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Folarin Balogun
images.caption: Folarin Balogun scored twice in the USMNT's World Cup opener against Paraguay.
images.credit: Cory Knowlton/ISI Photos/ISI Photos via Getty Images

images.name: Ousmane Dembele [1296x729]
images.caption: Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat trick against Norway in France's final group game, catapulting him into the Golden Boot race.

images.name: Vini Jr Brazil
images.caption: Vinícius Júnior has been Brazil's standout star so far this tourament.

article.headline: Austria and Algeria draw 3-3 to advance to KOs, send Iran home
article.description: Iran would have advanced as one of the eight best third-place teams had Austria or Algeria won.

article.story:
KANSAS CITY, Mo. -- Austria and Algeria played out a thrilling 3-3 draw Saturday night in what amounted to a win-win result in their World Cup group-stage finale, allowing both to advance to the knockout round while eliminating Iran from the tournament.

The game was tied 2-all in the closing minutes, and Algeria looked as if it was content to run out the clock, when captain Riyad Mahrez scored his second goal with about a minute left in stoppage time. That put Austria on the verge of elimination, only for Sasa Kalajdzic to head home the equalizing goal moments later and rescue Austria's World Cup hopes.

Marko Arnautovic and Marcel Sabitzer scored the first two goals for Austria, who finished second behind Argentina in Group J to advance for the first time since 1982. Their reward is a matchup with European champions Spain on Thursday in Los Angeles.

Rafik Belghali also scored for Les Fennecs, who became the ninth of 10 teams from Africa to advance. They finished third in the group but get a Round of 32 showdown with Switzerland on Thursday night in Vancouver, British Columbia.

Iran would have advanced as one of the eight best third-place teams had Austria or Algeria won.
